channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
item.description: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

(Image credit: Michal Ruprecht for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Opinion: Remembering Barney Frank, trailblazing public servant
item.description: Mass. congressman Barney Frank was the first House member to come out as gay and was instrumental in Wall Street reforms after the Great Recession. He died this week at the age of 86.
item.content:encoded:
Mass. congressman Barney Frank was the first House member to come out as gay and was instrumental in Wall Street reforms after the Great Recession. He died this week at the age of 86.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Chile's MAGA-inspired border control
item.description: Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.
item.content:encoded:
Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.

(Image credit: John Bartlett)
item.dc:creator: John Bartlett

item.title: Summer electric bills sizzle as the cost of cooling climbs
item.description: The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: One solution for Maine's struggling fishing industry? Give fillets away for free
item.description: Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.
item.content:encoded:
Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.

(Image credit: Ryan David Brown for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: 15 years since a deadly tornado brought Joplin, Mo. together, kindness carries on
item.description: Nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild and a spirit of community service continues to this day. Researchers studying human behavior catastrophes can bring out compassion in surprising ways.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild and a spirit of community service continues to this day. Researchers studying human behavior catastrophes can bring out compassion in surprising ways.

(Image credit: FEMA)
item.dc:creator: Pauline Bartolone

item.title: 'I'd wait forever, but 334 days is crazy.' USS Ford finally comes home
item.description: The USS Ford came home to a hero's welcome. Sailors had been away from home for nearly a year, through two conflicts, a fire and problems with the sewage system.
item.content:encoded:
The USS Ford came home to a hero's welcome. Sailors had been away from home for nearly a year, through two conflicts, a fire and problems with the sewage system.

(Image credit: Mike Kropf)
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Coal mine gas explosion in China kills 90 people, state media say
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 90 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on duty at the time.
item.content:encoded:
A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 90 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on duty at the time.

(Image credit: Cao Yang)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
item.description: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration to force foreigners in the U.S. to apply for a green card abroad
item.description: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.
item.content:encoded:
Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
